street_blackboard.captain_narrative_draft:
## West Sunset Boulevard — Corridor Synthesis (47 points, 2,319 m)

**Overall character.** A mature, untreated HMA arterial. The driving surface across the entire corridor is the same generation of oxidized grey asphalt concrete, present already in 2016 imagery and unchanged in 2025 — no overlay, slurry, microsurface, or mill-and-overlay event in the 9-year arc. Maintenance has been reactive only: targeted crack-seal beads in wheelpaths and small utility-cut patches concentrated at intersections and manhole collars. The corridor is squarely in late Phase-2 of the deterioration S-curve, a high-value preservation candidate that has been deferred past its optimal treatment window.

**Tier distribution along the centerline.** The eastern and central 1,950 m (pts 1–39) are stable **Fair** — wheelpath longitudinals 5–10 mm, scattered transverse cracks, light raveling, faded markings, no alligator and no rutting. Cross-witnessed 2016↔2025 evidence shows slow, predictable progression with crack-seal maintenance largely keeping the surface from tipping into Poor. **Two distress hotspots break the pattern.** The east-end terminus (**pt 0**) shows medium alligator in 2016 imagery (no 2025 capture available within radius) — Poor by Critical Rule 2. A more significant **west-end Poor cluster runs ~250 m from pt 40 through pt 45** (pts 40, 41, 42, 44, 45 = Poor; pt 43 a Fair island between intersections), characterized by medium alligator in wheelpaths and at signalized intersection approaches, edge breaks, and a complete absence of crack-seal maintenance. Pt 41 alligator was already chronic in 2016 — 9+ years untreated. Pt 46 (corridor west terminus) recovers to Fair. A single **Good** point at pt 4 reflects a tree-shadowed local minimum of distress, not a treatment boundary.

**Treatment history.** No corridor-wide preservation event in the observed window. The crack-seal+patch maintenance regime is active across roughly pts 7–38 (intermittent, not continuous) but conspicuously **absent from the west-end Poor cluster (pts 40–45)** — suggesting maintenance jurisdiction or scheduling boundary rather than condition-driven priority. Several manhole collars (notably pts 7, 22, 28, 43) show settled or radially-cracked patches that have been chronic since 2016.

**Findings for the engineer.** (1) **West-end Poor cluster (pts 40–42, 44–45, ~2000–2250 m)** is the dispatch priority — chronic untreated alligator at signalized intersection approaches, braking-load concentration, accelerating toward pothole emergence. Recommend mill-and-overlay; preservation window has closed. (2) **Pt 0 east terminus** Poor — flag for verification, only 2016 imagery available, current state unknown. (3) **Pt 18 watch-item** — incipient alligator forming in right wheelpath in 2025 (absent in 2016) with no crack-seal applied; trending toward Poor. (4) **The remaining ~38 Fair points are a single corridor-scale microsurfacing/thin-overlay candidate** — treating now (PCI ~55–65) preserves $4–7/$1 cost ratio vs deferring to mill-and-overlay. (5) **Manhole collar repairs** at pts 7, 22, 28, 43 are independent of pavement scope but chronic and worth bundling. No `unknown` grades; cross-witnessing was clean across the corridor.

synthesis.treatment_history_summary: The corridor has received only reactive maintenance over the 2016→2025 window: targeted crack-sealing on longitudinal/transverse cracks across most mid-corridor points (1–17, 19–38) and scattered utility-cut patches around manholes and gutter edges. Conspicuously absent are any surface-renewal treatments — no mill-and-overlay, no slurry seal, no microsurfacing, and no chip seal anywhere along the 2.3 km. The same oxidized AC surface generation has aged in place for 9+ years. The west-end zone (pts 40–45) and isolated point 18 also missed even the crack-seal pass, which correlates directly with where the corridor has now failed structurally.
synthesis.corridor_narrative_short:
**Tier distribution:** 6 Poor (pts 0, 40, 41, 42, 44, 45), 39 Fair, 1 Good (pt 4), 1 Fair-with-watch (pt 18). The corridor is overwhelmingly stable-Fair through the middle, with two failure clusters — a single Poor point at the east terminus and a contiguous 5-point Poor zone (pts 40–45, ~250 m) near the west end.

**Standout finding:** The west-end Poor zone is chronic, not progressive — 2016 imagery shows alligator already present at pt 41, meaning this segment has been left untreated for 9+ years while crack-seal was applied to neighboring points. The defect concentrates at signalized intersection stop-bar approaches (pts 41, 44, 45), where braking loads accelerate fatigue. Pt 40 has progressed from Fair-with-sealed-cracks (2016) to high-severity alligator with edge-stripe disruption (2025) — a clear preservation-failure case.

**Treatment sequence:** (1) Immediate: deep-patch or mill-and-overlay the west-end zone (pts 40–45) and pt 0; (2) Scheduled: corridor-wide crack-seal + microsurfacing/thin overlay across the Fair middle before more points migrate to Poor; (3) Bring pt 18 back into the maintained envelope with a targeted crack-seal pass. The $1-now / $4-later economics strongly favor acting before next winter.
